name: A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR
description: Dual ADSR envelope generator with retrigger inputs, a shared CV time input per channel, and jumper-selectable normal/inverted second outputs. Gate 1 can be normalled to Gate 2.

controls.name: Attack
controls.description: Sets rise time for the envelope.

controls.name: Decay
controls.description: Sets fall from attack peak to sustain level.

controls.name: Sustain
controls.description: Sets sustain level while gate stays high.

controls.name: Release
controls.description: Sets fall time after gate closes.

inputs.name: Retrig 1
inputs.description: Retriggers envelope 1 while gate remains high.

inputs.name: Gate 2
inputs.description: Gate input for envelope 2. Can be normalled from Gate 1 by jumper.

inputs.name: Retrig 2
inputs.description: Retriggers envelope 2 while gate remains high.

outputs.name: Output 2
outputs.description: Normal or inverted envelope output for envelope 1, selected by jumper.

outputs.name: Output 4
outputs.description: Normal or inverted envelope output for envelope 2, selected by jumper.

behaviors.name: Gate bus normalisation
behaviors.description: Gate 1 also starts envelope 2. That makes the module behave like a paired dual ADSR until the jumper is removed.

behaviors.name: Retrigger while open
behaviors.trigger: pulse Retrig while Gate stays high
behaviors.description: The current envelope restarts without needing a new gate low-high cycle. Useful for ratchets and repeated accents.

behaviors.name: Time CV routing by jumper
behaviors.description: Internal jumpers decide whether CVT acts on A, D, S, R, and whether rising CV shortens or stretches those stages.

behaviors.name: Inverted second output
behaviors.description: The second output can be normal or inverted, which is convenient for paired VCA/filter movement from one envelope source.

behaviors.name: Bus gate access
behaviors.description: The module can receive gate from the A-100 bus instead of only the front-panel gate jack.

patchIdeas.name: Paired VCA envelopes
patchIdeas.patch: Gate 1 from keyboard or sequencer drives both ADSRs via the normalled gate bus; Output 1 opens a VCA, Output 4 opens a second voice, and the inverted output shapes a complementary filter envelope. The two voices stay locked but move differently.
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR:
* Gate 1 -> Gate 2 normalled
* Output 2 = inverted

Keyboard:
VCA 1:
VCA 2:
VCO 1:
VCO 2:
Filter:
Mixer:

- Keyboard (Gate) g> A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Gate 1)
- Keyboard (Pitch) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Keyboard (Pitch) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Output 1) >> VCA 1 (CV)
- A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Output 4) >> VCA 2 (CV)
- A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Output 2) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- VCO 1 (Out) -> VCA 1 (In)
- VCO 2 (Out) -> VCA 2 (In)
- VCA 1 (Out) -> Mixer (In 1)
- VCA 2 (Out) -> Mixer (In 2)

patchIdeas.name: Retrig percussion
patchIdeas.patch: A short gate opens the first ADSR, and a faster trigger train into Retrig 1 restarts the decay while the note is still held. The result is a single envelope that can spit ratcheted accents without extra logic.
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR:
* Time Range: M

Clock:
Envelope:
VCA:

- Keyboard (Gate) g> A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Gate 1)
- Clock (Out) t> A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Retrig 1)
- A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Output 1) >> Envelope (Trig)
- A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Output 1) >> VCA (CV)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- Noise (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Slow dual contour
patchIdeas.patch: CVT is jumpered to A, D, and R on both channels, with the attenuator set low and the time range on H. A slow LFO into CVT lengthens and shortens both envelopes together for long drone swells and fades.
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR:
* Time Range: H
* CVT affects: A+D+R

LFO:
VCO:
Filter:
VCA:

- LFO (Out) >> A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (CVT 1)
- LFO (Out) >> A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (CVT 2)
- A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Output 1) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Output 4) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Inverted filter pair
patchIdeas.patch: Output 1 drives the amp while Output 2, set inverted by jumper, drives the filter cutoff in the opposite direction. The same gate creates a louder note with a closing filter sweep instead of the usual opening sweep.
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR:
* Output 2 = inverted

VCO:
Filter:
VCA:

- Sequencer (Gate) g> A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Gate 1)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Output 1) >> VCA (CV)
- A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Output 2) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Manual dual pulse
patchIdeas.patch: A fingered gate into Gate 2 leaves envelope 1 free for the main note while envelope 2 is used as a manual accent or percussion contour. The two ADSRs can run with the same trigger source or with separate timings for call-and-response patches.
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR:
* Gate 1 -> Gate 2 normalled off

Clock:
Button:
VCO:
VCA:
Envelope:

- Clock (Out) g> A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Gate 1)
- Button (Gate) g> A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Gate 2)
- A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Output 1) >> VCA (CV)
- A-140-2 Dual Mini ADSR (Output 3) >> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)
